summary: Ronacher revisits his 2022 “As-Any Hack,” which worked around Rust’s lack of supertrait upcasting for `dyn DebugAny`. The core pain was that a `dyn DebugAny` couldn’t be treated as `dyn Any`, so `downcast_ref` and related `Any` APIs failed at compile time. He reports that Rust 1.86 finally fixes this, enabling the cast and making those downcasts work on trait objects that inherit `Any`. The fix is currently in beta, with stable imminent, which means old workaround patterns can be retired once MSRV advances. He closes with a thanks to contributors and provides an updated extension map example that no longer needs the hack.
key_points: Trait object upcasting to supertraits (`DebugAny` → `Any`) was previously blocked on stable Rust.
The inability to call `Any` methods on `dyn DebugAny` broke common downcasting patterns.
Rust 1.86 adds the missing upcast support, making the cast and `downcast_ref` work.
This change obsoletes prior hacks once projects can bump MSRV.
A cleaned-up extension map demonstrates the new, simpler approach.
money_quotes: That hack is a way to get upcasting to supertraits working on stable Rust.
The problem? Even though `DebugAny` inherits from `Any`, Rust wouldn't let you use methods from `Any` on a `dyn DebugAny`.
So while you could call `DebugAny` methods just fine, trying to use `downcast_ref` from `Any` (the reason to use Any in the first place) would fail.
But there is good news! As of Rust 1.86, this is finally fixed.
That means a lot of old hacks can finally be retired.
Thank you so much to everyone who worked on this to make it work!
key_insight: Rust 1.86 finally enables trait-object upcasting to supertraits like `Any`, removing long-standing hacks and restoring ergonomic downcasting.
